relative_path: Protocols, instructions/Tissue dissociation, organoids and iPDCs/LIVE-DYE staining.docx
full_text:
LIVE-DYE staining

Organoids cultured on a 384-wel or 96-welll plate, embedded in omentum gel are stained against live/dye as follows:

For 384-well plate, take 3x 5 mL (in 15 mL falcons) of 4% paraformaldehyde to RT to melt. Falcons are in the bottom drawer of -20C freezer in the lab.

For 96 wells, calculate depending on the number of wells in the experiment.

For 384-well plate, prepare 8 mL of Fixable Live-or-Dye™ staining (Biotium, #32004) with a dilution of 1:1000 in sterile 1x PBS.

For 96 wells, calculate depending on the number of wells in the experiment.

Aliquots of the stain are in -20C lab freezer, in box 3. Each aliquot is 2 uL. Keep aliquots protected from light!

Prepare the staining dilution into a disposable 25 mL reservoir.

Remove the cell culture medium by reverting the plate on top of tissue paper (regular tissue paper from over the sink). Tap the plate a couple of times onto the paper on the bench. Dry the plate with white microscope tissue (kimtech or non-lint).

For 384 well plate: Using the digital multichannel dispenser and 300 uL filtered tips, wash cells with once with 70 uL sterile 1x PBS and add 20 uL of the 1:1000 dilution of live-dye stain per well.

For 96 well plate: Using multi-channel pipette, add 80ul of the 1:1000 diluted live-dye per well

For 384-well plate, spin the plate 300 g for 5 min, RT. Use cell culture centrifuge: change the rotor for the plate holder and spin (always make sure the rotor is tightened properly before use!). No need to spin 96-well-plate.

Incubate cells for 30 minutes at room temperature, protected from light.

After the incubation, add 70 uL sterile 1x PBS per well (384-well), or 100uL/well (96-well) and remove the PBS.

Fix cells in 4% paraformaldehyde (50 uL for 384, and 100uL for 96-wells) for 40 minutes at room temperature, protected from light.

Wash cells 3x with 1x PBS, (70 uL for 384, and 100uL for 96-wells)

After washing, leave 70 uL for 384, and 200uL for 96-well plate of 1x PBS per well. Seal the plate lid with parafilm, and cover the plate with tin foil. Mark the tin foil with Sample ID, staining, date and initials (e.g. S150_pOme; live-dye; 1.8.2022 eap). Place the plate inside +4C fridge.
